title: std::exception with bad Hive credentials
description:
### Describe the bug
Easy to reproduce. It seems to be missing input sanitization.
### How to reproduce
Run:
```sql
CREATE DATABASE d0 ENGINE = DataLakeCatalog('myminio', 'mypassword') SETTINGS catalog_type = 'hive', warehouse = 'myware', storage_endpoint = 'http://minio1:9001/d0';
SELECT 1 FROM merge(REGEXP('d0.*'), 't0'); --std::exception
```
### Error message and/or stacktrace
Stack trace:

bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INTEGER, name VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, value DOUBLE PRECISION);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name, value) VALUES (1, 'test1', 10.5), (2, 'test2', NULL);
CREATE SCHEMA IF NOT EXISTS test_db;
SELECT id, name, value FROM test_table WHERE value IS NULL;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE name LIKE 'test%');
key_bug_features: Missing input sanitization for Hive credentials
